CT Debates Risks and Benefits of Doxxing
For Doxxing:
> Undoxxed with no reason is seen as outdated in 2026.
> Builders, content creators, and career-focused people benefit from visibility, credibility, and networking.
> Founders hiding identity raises trust concerns; doxxing signals accountability.
> Helps with onboarding mainstream audiences through traditional platforms like YouTube/Instagram.
Against Doxxing:
> Increases the risk of targeted theft, extortion, kidnapping
> Being your own bank is only a real advantage if you stay anonymous; a known face makes you a target
> Database leaks are growing and AI tools make identity exposure increasingly dangerous over time
> Even being publicly known in any capacity makes you a physical target, risk no longer worth reward.

The Secret History of Polymarket – Part 1
This article explores the origins of Polymarket, suggesting it is a privatized rebranding of the Pentagon's controversial, defunct "Terrorism Futures Market" (the Policy Analysis Market)
Backed by prominent tech billionaires, platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi are expanding into mainstream finance and politics while facing growing concerns over market manipulation and insider trading
https://unlimitedhangout.com/2026/06/investigative-series/the-secret-history-of-polymarket-part-1/

MYSTERIOUS SOLANA PROJECT WORLD UNVEILED AS FULLY ONCHAIN PREDICTION MARKET
SOURCE

The World as Model - on sensing, representations, and the financialization of the physical world
Just as high-frequency trading firms won by building high-fidelity representations of market data, a new generation of companies is capturing physical infrastructure at its native resolution to create a digital representation layer
Mapping reality through physics-informed models instead of linguistic bottlenecks allows these platforms to accurately predict asset behavior and reshape how trillions of dollars in industrial assets are financed, insured, and traded
https://cruciblecapital.substack.com/p/the-world-as-model

CT Debates Risks and Benefits of Doxxing
For Doxxing:
> Undoxxed with no reason is seen as outdated in 2026.
> Builders, content creators, and career-focused people benefit from visibility, credibility, and networking.
> Founders hiding identity raises trust concerns; doxxing signals accountability.
> Helps with onboarding mainstream audiences through traditional platforms like YouTube/Instagram.
Against Doxxing:
> Increases the risk of targeted theft, extortion, kidnapping
> Being your own bank is only a real advantage if you stay anonymous; a known face makes you a target
> Database leaks are growing and AI tools make identity exposure increasingly dangerous over time
> Even being publicly known in any capacity makes you a physical target, risk no longer worth reward.

Singapore remained one of the world’s wealthiest economies. Average wealth per adult reached US$527,217 (S$683,600)
The number of US-dollar millionaires here also grew 2.2 per cent – or 5,240 people – to 244,000
https://www.straitstimes.com/business/singapores-us-dollar-millionaires-rise-to-244000-as-global-wealth-hits-record-high-ubs

Venice + Grass
Token Structure Debate
Summary of CT discussions on GRASS and VVV, two AI projects at the center of the token structure debate
1. GRASS
a)
Debate FOR Grass
• An ownerless foundation that owns the IP and network with token as the primary economic unit; recent raises were token-only, and reinvesting revenue in growth (rather than 100% buybacks) is the team's choice for a high-growth business
• Reached profitability and now paying network contributors directly in USDC from real revenue, transition from token subsidies to a self-sustaining economy where participants are compensated for creating actual economic value
b) Debate AGAINST Grass
• "Ownerless foundation” framing may be misleading - centralized decisions control revenue allocation (but can argue that Hyperliquid is the same), and the token lacks protections or value accrual
• Paying contributors in USDC diverts real revenue away from token holders with minimal/no buybacks, GRASS has no enforceable claim on cash flows or ownership
———
2. VVV (Venice)
a)
Debate FOR VVV
• Equity holders are the largest VVV token holders, creating strong alignment, while the token delivers real utility (Pro access, DIEM minting for compute credits) and benefits from revenue-backed buybacks and burns
• The dual structure allows equity to handle real-world business (AI lab deals, capital raises), while routing value back to token holders via burns
b) Debate AGAINST VVV
• Equity controls governance, cash flows, and all infrastructure while the token only receives discretionary burns and utility perks, repeating the classic failed dual model where token holders have no ownership claim on the business
• Even when equity holds tokens, incentives favor equity (they keep the real upside and can treat tokens as junior/exit liquidity), giving the token near-zero terminal value regardless of business success

The Secret History of Polymarket – Part 1
This article explores the origins of Polymarket, suggesting it is a privatized rebranding of the Pentagon's controversial, defunct "Terrorism Futures Market" (the Policy Analysis Market)
Backed by prominent tech billionaires, platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi are expanding into mainstream finance and politics while facing growing concerns over market manipulation and insider trading
https://unlimitedhangout.com/2026/06/investigative-series/the-secret-history-of-polymarket-part-1/

June '26: The Marks Came Back (TVPI). The Cash Didn't (DPI)
VC valuations and paper gains (TVPI) have strongly recovered, but actual cash distributions to investors (DPI) remain stagnant due to a choked exit market
Consequently, funds that proactively engineer liquidity through secondary markets and partial exits will hold a distinct advantage when trying to raise capital from increasingly impatient investors
https://benlakoff.substack.com/p/june-26-the-marks-came-back-tvpi

Venice Raises a $65M Series A at a $1B Valuation
> Venice has raised its first-round of outside capital: $65m at a valuation of $1 billion.
> The round was led by Dragonfly, alongside North Island Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Archetype, Liquid2 Ventures, Morgan Creek, and others.
> Venice aims to construct the platform dedicated to private and unrestricted machine intelligence; an open, permissive port city that respects the sovereignty of its inhabitants, human & agentic.
Source:
https://x.com/ErikVoorhees/status/2072326370206581037

Former Binance CFO Wei on Why Crypto is Still in a Bear Market and When Bull Market
>
On bear:
“ALL the whales got nuked going into Uptober on Oct 10/11. Lack of transparency from Binance, so people still dont 100% trust their system wont crash again if Trump makes another statement on a weekend”
>
When bull:
When Google and Facebook launch their own blockchain for devs to launch tokens.. Clarity Act passes, and  China loosens its noose

https://x.com/thedaoofwei/status/2072289037885260019

Recent Joe Rogan podcast dunks on crypto for ~ 5 minutes
"Democrat fund creators for political influence, and that crypto serves as a vehicle for money laundering, covert payments, and intelligence operations alongside legitimate projects. The overlap between crypto, intelligence agencies, and criminal syndicates makes it nearly impossible to distinguish legitimate projects from scams designed for influence or fraud."
Crypto losing mindshare/trust among the mainstream audiences
Link

Collector Crypt's $62M revenue is the same money, going in circles
Collector Crypt’s reported $62M revenue was almost entirely ($58M) recycled external deposits looped through automated buyback bots, not organic demand - evidenced by 93% volume from top wallets and metronome-like bots making identical $250 buys 20 hours/day
The vault holds real graded cards worth $18.6M and recycling was disclosed, but revenue equals the float with near-zero net value creation
https://x.com/johna2an/status/2071106458695901425?s=20

Modern tech substitutes like streaming and AI chatbots have created a synthetic form of connection, accelerating a widespread public health crisis of loneliness
Overcoming this isolation requires people to embrace the vulnerability of real-world interactions and actively rebuild genuine communities through scheduled, in-person social groups
https://jords.life/p/counterfeit-congregation

AI Chip Startup “Etched” Emerges from Stealth with $800M Raised and $1B in AI Chip Contracts  > Etched has emerged from stealth after raising $800M & securing over $1B in customer contracts, with its first AI inference racks set to ship this summer after successful…

AI Chip Startup “Etched” Emerges from Stealth with $800M Raised and $1B in AI Chip Contracts
> Etched has emerged from stealth after raising $800M & securing over $1B in customer contracts, with its first AI inference racks set to ship this summer after successful early performance tests.
> The company also unveiled Low-Voltage Inference (LVI) and Cluster-Scale Memory (CSM), two chip architecture innovations designed to deliver state-of-the-art AI inference throughput, latency, and power efficiency for frontier AI models.
Source:
https://x.com/Etched/status/2071972062202343590?s=20

The AI Industry Is Losing
Massive trillion-dollar AI CAPEX boom represents a high-risk financial bubble that outpaces actual corporate cash flows and primarily serves to prop up deeply unprofitable model labs like OpenAI and Anthropic
If tech giants pull back on this aggressive data center and semiconductor spending due to disappointing returns, it could trigger a severe investment bust with catastrophic knock-on effects across heavily leveraged tech companies and financial creditors
https://www.wheresyoured.at/the-ai-industry-is-losing/
